updates.title: Equity vs debt mutual fund taxation: How your MF gains are taxed in India
updates.summary: Mutual fund taxation in India varies by fund type and holding period. Equity funds face different tax rates for short-term and long-term gains, while debt funds purchased after April 1, 2023, are taxed at income tax slab rates. Details here.
updates.user_impact: May affect your tax liability or filing process
updates.content_angle: Educational explainer on this taxation development

updates.title: ITR: Here's why it is important to ensure TDS statement, Form 26AS details match before you file income-tax returns
updates.summary: It is important to ensure that details on your TDS statements (Form 16 and 16A) and financial statement Form 26AS match before you complete your income-tax returns so as to ensure smooth filing and refund process.

updates.title: How are profits from intraday and Futures & Options trading taxed in India? Explained
updates.summary: In India, intraday trading profits are taxed as speculative business income, while Futures &amp; Options trading profits are non-speculative. Here are the income tax rules.

updates.title: Sweep-in fixed deposit facility: A smart way to partially withdraw from FD and earn higher returns
updates.summary: A sweep-in facility is a feature that helps you earn better returns on excess funds lying idle in your savings or current account. Under this facility, any sum in your savings account above a pre-set limit is automatically transferred into an FD.
